text.title: Judge Calibration Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for calibrating an LLM-as-judge before it gates downstream actions.

rule.statement: Calibration runs against ≥30 hand-labelled cases. Below this κ confidence intervals are too wide to act on.
rule.rationale: For binary labels with 30 cases, the 95% CI on κ is typically ±0.18; below 30 the CI swallows the threshold.

rule.statement: A judge ships only if κ ≥ 0.7 against the holdout. Below that the judge is not better than coarse heuristic baselines.
rule.rationale: κ ≥ 0.7 corresponds to "substantial agreement" (Landis-Koch 1977); production gates that depend on the judge need at least this floor.

rule.statement: The calibration report logs full confusion matrix + false-pass rate + false-fail rate, not just the headline κ.
rule.rationale: κ aggregates errors symmetrically; an asymmetric production cost (false-pass much worse than false-fail) needs the per-cell view to choose a threshold or modify the prompt.

rule.statement: Recalibrate on every judge-prompt change, judge-model upgrade, label-policy change, or quarterly — whichever comes first.
rule.rationale: Judge prompts and models drift; static κ measured once becomes obsolete and the gate quietly degrades.

rule.statement: Holdout cases must not appear (verbatim or as examples) in the judge prompt or training data of the judge model. The fixture is independent of the prompt.
rule.rationale: Contamination inflates κ to near-1 on the holdout while real-world κ stays low; the gate then trusts a fake measurement.

rule.statement: When stakes are high (safety, customer-facing) holdout labels come from ≥2 independent raters with disagreements resolved by a third. Single-rater labels are noted as preliminary and re-checked.
rule.rationale: Single-rater labels carry the rater's bias; multi-rater reveals label disagreements that bound the achievable κ.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → execute content/04-procedure.xml and produce a calibration-report.json passing the validator.
Advisory judge or no labelable cases → skip and use pairwise comparison or human-only review.
